Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Analyze the Event Log I need to go through each case 1001 1002 1003 1004 and list the activities in chronological order based on the timestamps Standard P2P Flow Create Requisition Approve Requisition Issue PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1001 Analysis PR Approve PO Receive Invoice Match Pay Close Verdict This looks like a standard healthy process All dependencies are respected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Analysis PO issued before PR approval Anomaly PO issued before PR approval Payment released before Goods received Anomaly Payment released before Goods received Payment released before Matching Anomaly Payment released before Matching Verdict Multiple severe anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Analysis Release Payment before Vendor Invoice Received Anomaly Payment before Invoice Receive Goods Anomaly Payment before Goods Match Invoice to PO Anomaly Payment before Matching Verdict Severe anomalies Payment released before any downstream events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Analysis Receive Goods at 0900 Issue PO at 0915 Anomaly Goods received before PO issued Verdict Goods received before PO

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I should categorize the anomalies by case for clarity For each anomaly I need to state what happened and why it is problematic based on business logic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: General P2P Principles to cite Segregation of Duties Authorization controls PR approval before PO Three way match PO Receipt Invoice before payment Payment should be the last step before close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: PO before PR Approval Bypasses budget check unauthorized spending Payment before Goods Paying for nothing Risk of fraud cash loss Payment before Match No validation of price quantity Risk of overpayment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment before Invoice Goods Match Extreme anomaly Pay without invoice Pay without receipt Complete lack of control

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Goods before PO Receipt without PO Unauthorized procurement Potential fraud Inventory risk

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check timestamps Did I read 1002 correctly 1002 PO 0201 0840 PR Approve 0203 0800 Yes PO is before Approve 1002 Payment 0203 0715 Receive Goods 0204 0900 Yes Payment before Goods 1002 Payment 0203 0715 Match 0205 0800 Yes Payment before Match 1003 Payment 0208 1100 Invoice 0209 1200 Yes Payment before Invoice 1004 Goods 0206 0900 PO 0206 0915 Yes Goods before PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For 1002 and 1003 emphasize Three Way Match failure The three way match is PO Goods Receipt Invoice If payment happens before any of these the match is impossible or bypassed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For 1004 emphasize that receiving goods creates an obligation If the PO is not there the company might not know what they agreed to pay or the goods might be wrong

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For 1002 Issue PO before Approve PR suggests the requester might have system access to issue POs without approval or an admin bypassed controls

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Final Polish Mental Outline of Response Introduction Define standard P2P flow briefly Case by Case Breakdown Case 1001 Normal Case 1002 Multiple anomalies Case 1003 Multiple anomalies Case 1004 One anomaly Summary of Risks Group by risk type Conclusion Recommend investigation
